terms.definition: The surgical removal of a tooth from its socket in the alveolar bone.
terms.exampleSentence: Atraumatic extraction of the lower molar was performed under local anaesthesia.
terms.contextNotes: Document tooth, technique, and post-op advice in writing.

terms.definition: The replacement of lost tooth structure with a dental material such as composite or amalgam.
terms.exampleSentence: A direct composite restoration was placed on the occlusal surface of the lower first molar.
terms.contextNotes: Use 'filling' in patient communication for clarity.

terms.definition: Endodontic treatment to remove infected pulp, disinfect the root canal system, and obturate it.
terms.exampleSentence: Root canal therapy was completed over two visits using rotary files.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid the lay term 'nerve treatment' in clinical notes.

terms.definition: The dental specialty concerned with the morphology, physiology, and pathology of the dental pulp and periapical tissues.
terms.exampleSentence: The case was referred to endodontics for management of a calcified canal.
terms.contextNotes: Adjective: endodontic.

terms.definition: The mechanical removal of plaque and calculus from tooth surfaces, both supragingivally and subgingivally.
terms.exampleSentence: Full-mouth scaling and oral hygiene instruction were completed.
terms.contextNotes: Often combined with root planing as 'SRP'.

terms.definition: Smoothing the root surfaces to remove residual calculus, plaque, and contaminated cementum.
terms.exampleSentence: Quadrant root planing was performed under local anaesthesia.
terms.contextNotes: Cornerstone of non-surgical periodontal therapy.

terms.definition: Preventive treatment, in dentistry typically the professional cleaning of teeth to remove plaque and stains.
terms.exampleSentence: Six-monthly dental prophylaxis was recommended for caries prevention.
terms.contextNotes: Antibiotic prophylaxis distinct concept for at-risk patients.

terms.definition: A full-coverage prosthetic restoration cemented over a prepared tooth or implant abutment.
terms.exampleSentence: A porcelain-fused-to-metal crown was cemented onto the lower right first molar.
terms.contextNotes: Patients often refer to a crown as a 'cap'.

terms.definition: A fixed prosthesis that replaces one or more missing teeth by anchoring to adjacent teeth or implants.
terms.exampleSentence: A three-unit bridge was placed to replace the missing upper first premolar.
terms.contextNotes: Cantilever and Maryland are common designs.

terms.definition: A removable prosthesis replacing some or all teeth and associated structures.
terms.exampleSentence: A new upper complete denture was fitted following healing.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid 'plate' in formal documentation.

terms.definition: A titanium fixture surgically placed in the alveolar bone to support a prosthetic tooth or denture.
terms.exampleSentence: The dental implant was placed using a guided surgical template.
terms.contextNotes: Osseointegration typically requires 3–6 months.

terms.definition: A thin layer of ceramic or composite bonded to the labial surface of a tooth to improve aesthetics.
terms.exampleSentence: Six porcelain veneers were placed on the upper anterior teeth.
terms.contextNotes: Minimal-prep techniques preserve enamel.

terms.definition: A tooth-coloured restorative material made of a resin matrix and inorganic filler particles.
terms.exampleSentence: A nanohybrid composite resin was selected for the anterior restoration.
terms.contextNotes: Bonded with phosphoric acid etch and adhesive.

terms.definition: A silver-coloured restorative alloy containing mercury, tin, silver, and copper.
terms.exampleSentence: The failing amalgam restoration was replaced with composite.
terms.contextNotes: Use phased down per Minamata Convention.

terms.definition: An aesthetic procedure that lightens tooth colour using peroxide-based agents.
terms.exampleSentence: Home tooth bleaching with 10% carbamide peroxide was prescribed.
terms.contextNotes: Sensitivity is a common transient side effect.

terms.definition: Topical application of a high-concentration fluoride coating to prevent caries and treat hypersensitivity.
terms.exampleSentence: Fluoride varnish application was provided as part of the child's caries-prevention plan.
terms.contextNotes: Recommended every 6 months for at-risk children.

terms.definition: A thin resin coating applied to occlusal pits and fissures to prevent caries.
terms.exampleSentence: Fissure sealants were placed on the newly erupted first permanent molars.
terms.contextNotes: Standard preventive measure for paediatric molars.

terms.definition: Removal of oral tissue for histopathological examination, typically of suspicious mucosal lesions.
terms.exampleSentence: An incisional biopsy of the lateral tongue lesion was performed under local anaesthesia.
terms.contextNotes: Indicated for any unexplained ulcer present > 3 weeks.

terms.definition: Surgical excision of gingival tissue, often to reduce pocket depth or improve aesthetics.
terms.exampleSentence: A gingivectomy was undertaken to expose the cervical margins of the upper incisors.
terms.contextNotes: Differentiate from gingivoplasty (reshaping).

terms.definition: Surgical removal of the apex of a tooth root, typically performed when conventional endodontics has failed.
terms.exampleSentence: An apicoectomy was performed on the upper lateral incisor with persistent periapical infection.
terms.contextNotes: Often combined with retrograde MTA filling.

terms.definition: Movement of teeth and jaws using appliances to correct malocclusion and improve function and aesthetics.
terms.exampleSentence: Fixed orthodontic treatment with brackets and archwires was commenced.
terms.contextNotes: Average duration 18–24 months for fixed appliances.

terms.definition: A routine professional dental cleaning combining calculus removal with polishing of tooth surfaces.
terms.exampleSentence: A six-monthly scale and polish was provided as part of routine maintenance.
terms.contextNotes: Common patient-facing terminology in the UK.

terms.definition: Removal of a failing existing restoration and placement of a new restoration to restore function.
terms.exampleSentence: Tooth restoration replacement was undertaken due to recurrent caries beneath the existing amalgam.
terms.contextNotes: Preserve as much sound tooth structure as possible.

terms.definition: The reinsertion of an avulsed tooth back into its socket, ideally within 30 minutes of trauma.
terms.exampleSentence: Replantation of the avulsed upper central incisor was performed in the emergency department.
terms.contextNotes: Followed by 2-week flexible splint and root canal therapy.

terms.definition: Temporary stabilisation of mobile or traumatised teeth by joining them to adjacent stable teeth.
terms.exampleSentence: A flexible composite-wire splint was placed for two weeks following replantation.
terms.contextNotes: Splint duration depends on injury type and trauma guidelines.
